# io.write_vcz { #tspaint.io.write_vcz }

```python
tspaint.io.write_vcz(ts, path)
```

Write a minimal **VCF-Zarr** store from a *mutated* tree sequence ‚Äî a lightweight stand-in for
``bio2zarr``'s ``vcf2zarr`` output, so a simulation can be fed through the zarr front ends.

Writes the core arrays that :func:`variants_from_zarr` / :func:`variant_data_from_zarr` /
:func:`sample_names_from_zarr` read ‚Äî ``call_genotype`` ``(variants, samples, ploidy=1)``,
``variant_position``, ``variant_allele``, ``variant_contig`` / ``contig_id``, ``sample_id`` and
``variant_ancestral_allele`` ‚Äî so it round-trips through :func:`tspaint.io.tsinfer` /
:func:`tspaint.io.singer`. Each sample **node** becomes one haploid sample ``"n<i>"``; biallelic
``0`` / ``1`` sites (e.g. from :func:`add_mutations`).

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**ts**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tskit](`tskit`).[TreeSequence](`tskit.TreeSequence`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A mutated tree sequence ‚Äî must carry sites (overlay them with :func:`add_mutations` first).

<code>[**path**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Output ``.vcz`` store path.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   ``path`` (for chaining).
